route.name: Land of the Ancients Overnighter
route.region: White Mountains / Deep Springs / Big Pine
route.description: Public GPX high-elevation White Mountains route with serious climbing, dry valleys, remote roads, and altitude exposure.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX high-elevation White Mountains route with serious climbing, dry valleys, remote roads, and altitude exposure.
route.bestSeason: Late spring to early fall; July requires strict heat, storm, and water-capacity screening.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX high-elevation White Mountains route with serious climbing, dry valleys, remote roads, and altitude exposure.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Dry Grandview start, water at Crooked Creek/Wyman Canyon, and no casual resupply; carry substantial capacity.
resources.food: No on-route resupply; use Big Pine/Bishop before and after.
resources.camping: Remote camping options need exact legal confirmation for the selected itinerary.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Do not ride without a water-capacity plan.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
Land of the Ancients is not gate-confirmed until White Mountains/Ancient Bristlecone access, remote camping legality, and July water sources are confirmed.

Land of the Ancients remains experienced-rider-only and not gate-confirmed until Ancient Bristlecone boundary camping, Crooked Creek/Wyman Canyon water, and July heat/thunderstorm checks are confirmed.
No exact overnight camp has been proven outside the Ancient Bristlecone Pine Forest National Protection Area closure boundary.
Inyo Stage 1 Fire Restrictions prohibit campfires/charcoal outside listed developed exemptions; stove-only compliance must be documented.
Grandview and Schulman/Ancient Bristlecone areas are dry; Crooked Creek and Wyman Canyon water are not officially verified for July 10-13.
NWS forecasts pulled July 2 do not cover the full July 10-13 target window and already show potential heat on the Deep Springs/Wyman side.
High elevation, exposure, thunderstorm, wind, smoke, and limited bailout remain material go/no-go risks.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Check the GPX against the Ancient Bristlecone Pine Area Closure order and maps.
Verify Crooked Creek/Wyman Canyon water or define a conservative full-carry plan.
Define legal camps that do not conflict with closure/fire restrictions.
Check GPX and intended camp against Ancient Bristlecone closure/restriction boundary.
Verify Crooked Creek/Wyman Canyon water or define a full carry.
Refresh Inyo fire, heat, smoke, and thunderstorm forecast before promotion.
Georeference the official closure map or obtain official GIS/turning-point coordinates, then prove chosen camp coordinates are outside the Ancient Bristlecone protected area.
Select exact legal overnight coordinates and document dispersed-camping legality at those points.
Refresh Inyo alerts, Ancient Bristlecone closure, Stage 1 fire order, and Inyo/Mono road status on July 9 or July 10.
Verify Crooked Creek and Wyman Canyon water within 24-48 hours or replace with a documented full-carry plan.
Pull NWS point forecasts for Grandview, high route, and Wyman/Deep Springs once they cover July 10-13; reject if heat, storm, wind, red flag, or smoke thresholds are exceeded.
Require stove-only cooking with a shutoff-valve stove and any required CA Campfire Permit; no campfires.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: 61 miles over 2-3 days is plausible only for experienced riders comfortable with altitude, heat, and no bailout.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Camp must be outside the Ancient Bristlecone boundary and verified against current Inyo closure/restriction language.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Inyo Stage 1 restrictions, July heat in Deep Springs, and afternoon thunderstorm risk require final refresh.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Ancient Bristlecone closure/restriction boundaries must be checked against GPX and intended camp.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Grandview is dry, Schulman Grove has no water per subagent notes, and Crooked Creek/Wyman Canyon filtering must be verified.

readinessEvidence.finding: Official Inyo alerts list Stage 1 Fire Restrictions and an Ancient Bristlecone Pine Area Closure active in 2026.
Ancient Bristlecone Pine Area Closure prohibits some uses in the Ancient Bristlecone Pine Forest National Protection Area; route/camp corridor must be checked against the closure order.
The route is remote with no casual resupply and a dry Grandview-area start; no official July water confirmation is recorded in this workspace.

readinessEvidence.finding: Subagent reported route is plausible but hard; it must avoid camping inside Ancient Bristlecone boundary and confirm water from Crooked Creek/Wyman Canyon.

Public route page says Grandview Campground is dry, no camping is permitted within Ancient Bristlecone Pine Forest boundaries, Crooked Creek/Wyman Canyon are water sources, and no route resupply exists.
